title: French Social Democracy: The Fourth Republic
author: Autumn Chen, modded by hellan

options.title: Élections législatives de 1946
options.subtitle: The first National Assembly of the Fourth Republic is to be elected.

content.content: The first National Assembly of the Fourth Republic is to be elected now.

content: The Constitution approved in October has been promulgated, ending the long constituent period that followed liberation. The
and
still govern together under Tripartisme, but their agreement conceals a struggle over which of them will direct the new Republic.
For us, the election follows an internal change almost as important as the constitutional one. Guy Mollet has displaced Daniel Mayer as secretary-general of the

, defeating the old leadership on a programme of renewed Marxist language and closer action with the Communists. Léon Blum remains the party’s principal public authority. A majority in the 627-seat Assembly requires 314 deputies.
The larger parties cancel one another out, and Paul Ramadier of the
is accepted as President of the Council.
The restored Tripartite cabinet commands 457 seats. Maurice Thorez returns as vice-president of the Council, while the
retains major responsibility for labour, reconstruction and social policy. The
remains in government rather than permit a purely Socialist-Communist cabinet.

This majority is large enough to withstand almost any parliamentary revolt. It does not resolve the disputes over wages and prices, relations with the Soviet Union, denominational education or colonial policy. The
has received the premiership because it is the only party both larger partners can temporarily tolerate, not because the election strengthened us.
Maurice Thorez and Georges Bidault both fail to secure the necessary votes. Vincent Auriol therefore asks Léon Blum to form a government.
Blum is invested by 575 of the 583 deputies voting, but he cannot reconcile the
and
. On 17 December he presents a cabinet composed entirely of the
. Every ministry is Socialist; the government itself rests on no Socialist majority.

The arrangement is explicitly provisional and will end once the presidency and the remaining institutions of the Fourth Republic are installed. In its few weeks of office, the cabinet approves the Monnet Plan and imposes a five per cent reduction in prices. The outbreak of fighting in Hanoi ensures that even this caretaker government will not be uneventful.

thorez_tripartite.title: Entrust Tripartisme to Maurice Thorez of the PCF?
thorez_tripartite.subtitle: PCF + SFIO + MRP (457/627).
thorez_tripartite.unavailableSubtitle:
PCF + SFIO + MRP (457/627).
The MRP will not accept a Communist President of the Council, and twenty-five of our own deputies refuse to vote for Thorez. Even unanimous SFIO support would leave him short without the Christian democrats.

workers_front.title: Form a Workers’ Front?
workers_front.subtitle: PCF + SFIO (284/627).
workers_front.unavailableSubtitle:
PCF + SFIO (284/627).
The two workers’ parties are thirty seats short of a majority. The election has made the Communists stronger than us, but it has not given the parliamentary left enough deputies to govern alone.

popular_front.title: Re-create the Popular Front?
popular_front.subtitle: PCF + SFIO + RGR (353/627).
popular_front.unavailableSubtitle:
PCF + SFIO + RGR (353/627).
The Radicals and UDSR will not enter a Communist-dominated cabinet. The common anti-fascist programme of 1936 no longer supplies an agreement on the Constitution, foreign policy or nationalisation.

third_force.title: Break with the PCF and form a Third Force coalition?
third_force.subtitle: SFIO + MRP + RGR + Moderates (416/627).
third_force.unavailableSubtitle:
SFIO + MRP + RGR + Moderates (416/627).
The arithmetic exists, but the coalition does not. Mollet has just won the party leadership while advocating unity of action with the Communists, and the other Resistance parties have not yet broken Tripartisme. A Third Force belongs to a later crisis.

republican_coalition.title: Refuse to form a government, letting an MRP-led Republican Coalition form.
republican_coalition.subtitle: MRP + RGR + Moderates + Others (343/627).

content: Georges Bidault of the
remains at the head of government.
The Christian democrats assemble the
, the conservative independents and enough non-aligned deputies to command 343 seats. The
returns to opposition rather than arbitrate between the Communists and Christian democrats.
Tripartisme has ended before the new Republic is fully installed. The government has a majority, but not much common programme: the Radicals remain anticlerical, the

retains its social-Catholic commitments, and the parliamentary right wants a firmer break with the institutions and policies of the liberation period. Mollet can denounce the coalition from the left. He cannot deny that our refusal made it possible.

content.content: Election figures and institutional chronology are based chiefly on the French National Assembly’s historical material and contemporary parliamentary group totals.
